title: glechoma hederacea subsp. hirsuta
intro: Glechoma hederacea subsp. hirsuta, commonly known as hairy creeping thyme or creeping thyme, is a subspecies of the Glechoma hederacea plant, a type of perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Glechoma hederacea subsp. hirsuta include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as a natural antimicrobial agent, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. It can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in cooking and as a garnish, adding a subtle flavor and aroma to various dishes.
